chapter: Chapter 8: Force and Laws of Motion

questions.question: State Newton’s First Law of Motion.
questions.answer: An object remains at rest or in uniform motion unless acted upon by an unbalanced external force.

questions.question: What is meant by the law of inertia?
questions.answer: Objects resist changes in their state of rest or motion.

questions.question: Define momentum.
questions.answer: Momentum is the product of mass and velocity.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of force?
questions.answer: Newton (N).

questions.question: Define one Newton.
questions.answer: One Newton is the force required to accelerate a mass of 1 kg by 1 m/s².

questions.question: State Newton’s Second Law of Motion.
questions.answer: The rate of change of momentum of an object is proportional to the applied force.

questions.question: What is the formula for force?
questions.answer: F = ma.

questions.question: Define acceleration.
questions.answer: Acceleration is the rate of change of velocity.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of acceleration?
questions.answer: m/s².

questions.question: What is the relationship between force and acceleration?
questions.answer: Force is directly proportional to acceleration.

questions.question: What is friction?
questions.answer: Friction is the force that opposes motion between two surfaces.

questions.question: Why do objects stop moving eventually?
questions.answer: Due to friction.

questions.question: State Newton’s Third Law of Motion.
questions.answer: For every action there is an equal and opposite reaction.

questions.question: Do action and reaction act on the same body?
questions.answer: No, they act on different bodies.

questions.question: What happens to momentum when mass increases?
questions.answer: Momentum increases if velocity remains constant.

questions.question: What happens to momentum when velocity increases?
questions.answer: Momentum increases.

questions.question: Which physical quantity measures inertia?
questions.answer: Mass.

questions.question: What happens when a car suddenly stops?
questions.answer: Passengers move forward due to inertia.

questions.question: Give one example of action-reaction pair.
questions.answer: Rocket propulsion.

questions.question: Why do swimmers push water backward?
questions.answer: To move forward due to reaction force.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of mass?
questions.answer: Kilogram (kg).

questions.question: What type of quantity is momentum?
questions.answer: Vector quantity.

questions.question: What type of quantity is force?
questions.answer: Vector quantity.

questions.question: What happens when friction is absent?
questions.answer: Objects continue moving indefinitely.

questions.question: Who discovered the laws of motion?
questions.answer: Sir Isaac Newton.
